content.question: To what extent do reductions in formal SEN classification (through stricter diagnostic thresholds or destigmatization efforts) risk displacing sorting and segregation into alternative channels (school choice, informal class-level tracking, vocational tracking) rather than reducing segregation overall?
content.reduction_path: Model the interaction between SEN classification policy, free school-choice rules, and informal tracking mechanisms using administrative data on school enrollments, classroom compositions, and secondary-track placements; simulate effects of counterfactual policy changes on both SEN classification rates and overall segregation indices.
